content.problemStatement:
You are given two positive integers NNN and KKK.

A positive integer XXX is called a **Good Number** if XXX can be written as i1 ‚à£ i2 ‚à£ ‚ãØ ‚à£ iMi_1\ |\ i_2\ |\ \cdots\ |\ i_Mi1‚Äã ‚à£ i2‚Äã ‚à£ ‚ãØ ‚à£ iM‚Äã ‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ, where K‚â§i1<i2<‚ãØ<iM<N+KK \le i_1 < i_2 < \cdots < i_M < N + KK‚â§i1‚Äã<i2‚Äã<‚ãØ<iM‚Äã<N+K and 1‚â§M‚â§N1 \le M \le N1‚â§M‚â§N.

Now your task is to answer QQQ queries of the following type:


L,RL, RL,R : count the number of **Good Numbers** between lll and rrr inclusive.
‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ Given two nonnegative integers aaa and bbb, a ‚à£ ba\ |\ ba ‚à£ b denotes the Bitwise OR operator of aaa and bbb.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, KKK and QQQ.
The next QQQ lines each contain 222 integers - the parameters LLL and RRR for this query.




Output Format
For each test case, for each query, count the number of good integers between LLL and RRR.
constraints: The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^{6}106
